title: Pilosella knappii
taxonomy: Pilosella knappii is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Pilosella, which includes several species of plants commonly known as false dandelions. Pilosella knappii is native to the Himalayas and is considered to be a rare and endangered species. The classification of Pilosella knappii is further broken down into the kingdom Plantae, phylum Magnoliophyta, class Magnoliopsida, and order Asterales. This classification helps to understand the evolutionary relationships and characteristics of the plant.

intro: Pilosella knappii, also known as Knapp's mouse-ear hawkweed, is a rare and endangered species of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in wound healing and the treatment of skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The bioactive constituents of Pilosella knappii include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and polyphenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant are infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat a range of health issues, including digestive problems and respiratory infections.
